chunk_summary: This section focuses on rehydration protocols for infants under 2 years with severe acute malnutrition (SAM), including cholera-specific exceptions and appropriate oral rehydration solutions (ORS) options for preterm, low birth weight, and malnourished infants.
learning_objectives: Understand rehydration strategies for infants with severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Identify cholera-specific exceptions in rehydration protocols for malnourished infants.
Learn appropriate oral rehydration solutions (ORS) for preterm and low birth weight infants.
Review facility-based management guidelines for malnourished children under 2 years.
key_facts: Children with SAM require specialized rehydration protocols to avoid fluid overload and electrolyte imbalances.
WHO recommends low-osmolarity ORS for rehydration in SAM, except in cases of cholera where standard ORS is preferred.
Cholera-specific ORS contains higher sodium concentrations to address severe dehydration caused by cholera.
Operational guidelines emphasize facility-based management for malnourished children, including monitoring hydration and electrolyte levels.
Preterm and low birth weight infants are at higher risk of dehydration and require tailored rehydration approaches.
reference_quote: WHO recommends low-osmolarity ORS for rehydration in children with severe acute malnutrition, except in cases of cholera where standard ORS is preferred. (Source: https://www.who.int/tools/elena/interventions/dehydration-sam)
document_key_topics: Rehydration protocols for SAM in infants
Management of preterm and low birth weight infants
